_note: Sample utterances shown as a live cue card during Foundations sessions only. Beginners can read these aloud. Plain, high-frequency clinical English.

categories.name: 1) Greeting & introducing yourself
phrases: Hello, my name is ___. I'm a medical student.
Nice to meet you. May I check your name, please?
How would you like me to call you?
Is it okay if I ask you some questions about how you're feeling?

categories.name: 2) Opening — let the patient talk
phrases: What brings you in today?
How can I help you today?
Tell me more about that.
Please go on, I'm listening.

categories.name: 3) Asking about the symptom (plain SOCRATES)
phrases: When did it start?
Where exactly do you feel it?
Can you describe what it feels like?
On a scale of one to ten, how bad is it?
Does anything make it better or worse?
Has it changed since it started?

categories.name: 4) Following up & checking
phrases: Could you say a little more about that?
Just to make sure I understand, you mean ___?
Has anything like this happened before?
Is there anything else that's been bothering you?

categories.name: 5) Showing empathy
phrases: That sounds really difficult.
I can see this is worrying you.
Thank you for telling me that.
It's understandable to feel that way.
Take your time, there's no rush.

categories.name: 6) Checking understanding & explaining simply
phrases: Let me make sure I've understood you correctly.
Would it help if I explained that in a simpler way?
Does that make sense so far?
Please stop me if anything is unclear.

categories.name: 7) Closing the conversation
phrases: Is there anything you'd like to ask me?
Do you have any concerns we haven't talked about?
Thank you for your time today.
I'd like to summarize what we've discussed.
